course.title: Tajik, Start to Fluent
course.subtitle: Everyday Tajik through 2,000 sequential sentences, building from absolute basics to natural, connected speech
course.heroLedeSuffix: A Glossika-style sequence: short, natural sentences building steadily from beginner basics to connected, fluent speech.

lessons.title: My Day
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: a simple daily routine

readingPassage.context: A very basic present-tense text reviewing daily routine vocabulary.

paragraphs.native: Ном ман Мадина аст.
paragraphs.en: My name is Madina.

paragraphs.native: Ман ҳар рӯз соати ҳафт бедор мешавам.
paragraphs.en: I wake up at seven every day.

paragraphs.native: Ман нонушта мехӯрам ва чой менӯшам.
paragraphs.en: I eat breakfast and drink tea.

paragraphs.native: Баъд ман ба мактаб меравам.
paragraphs.en: Then I go to school.

paragraphs.native: Дар мактаб ман бо дӯстонам бозӣ мекунам.
paragraphs.en: At school I play with my friends.

paragraphs.native: Соати дувоздаҳ мо хӯроки пешин мехӯрем.
paragraphs.en: At twelve we eat lunch.

paragraphs.native: Баъд аз мактаб ман ба хона бармегардам.
paragraphs.en: After school I return home.

paragraphs.native: Ман вазифаамро иҷро мекунам.
paragraphs.en: I do my homework.

paragraphs.native: Шаб ман бо оилаам шом мехӯрам.
paragraphs.en: At night I have dinner with my family.

paragraphs.native: Ман хеле хаста мешавам ва мехобам.
paragraphs.en: I get very tired and go to sleep.

readingPassage.context: Basic present-tense description text, useful for practicing possessive and location vocabulary.

readingPassage.context: Basic present-tense narrative practicing shopping and family vocabulary.

paragraphs.native: Ман бо модарам ба бозор меравам.
paragraphs.en: I go to the market with my mother.

paragraphs.en: There are many people at the market.

paragraphs.en: The price of fruit is cheap.

paragraphs.en: My mother talks with the seller.

paragraphs.en: I carry the bag of fruit.

paragraphs.native: Мо инчунин нон мехарем.
paragraphs.en: We also buy bread.

paragraphs.native: Баъд аз бозор мо ба хона меравем.
paragraphs.en: After the market we go home.

paragraphs.native: Ман бозорро дӯст медорам, зеро он серодам аст.
paragraphs.en: I love the market because it's lively.

paragraphs.native: Модарам мегӯяд, ки ин бозор беҳтарин аст.

lessons.title: Meet Alisher
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: a teacher introduces himself and his family

readingPassage.context: A short self-introduction of the kind used throughout the course to review present-tense statements about work, family, and daily routine.

paragraphs.native: Салом! Номи ман Алишер аст.
paragraphs.en: Hello! My name is Alisher.

paragraphs.native: Ман сию панҷ сола ҳастам ва дар Душанбе зиндагӣ мекунам.
paragraphs.en: I am thirty-five years old and I live in Dushanbe.

paragraphs.native: Ман муаллим ҳастам ва ба кӯдакон забони англисӣ меомӯзонам.
paragraphs.en: I am a teacher, and I teach English to children.

paragraphs.native: Ҳар субҳ соати ҳафт мехезам, наҳорӣ мехӯрам ва бо автобус ба мактаб меравам.
paragraphs.en: Every morning I get up at seven, have breakfast, and go to school by bus.

paragraphs.native: Кори ман хеле ҷолиб аст, зеро ман бо бисёр одамон вомехӯрам.
paragraphs.en: My job is very interesting because I meet a lot of people.

paragraphs.native: Хонаводаи ман хурд аст: ман, занам ва ду фарзандамон.
paragraphs.en: My family is small: me, my wife, and our two children.

paragraphs.native: Занам духтур аст ва дар беморхона кор мекунад.
paragraphs.en: My wife is a doctor and works at a hospital.

paragraphs.native: Фарзандони мо дар мактаб мехонанд.
paragraphs.en: Our children study at school.

paragraphs.native: Онҳо ба варзиш шавқ доранд — писарам футбол бозӣ мекунад ва духтарам теннис дӯст медорад.
paragraphs.en: They're interested in sports — my son plays football and my daughter likes tennis.

paragraphs.native: Имрӯз ҳаво хуб аст, офтобист, лекин дирӯз борон борид ва хунук буд.
paragraphs.en: Today the weather is nice and sunny, but yesterday it rained and was cold.

paragraphs.native: Бегоҳирӯзӣ мо одатан якҷоя шом мехӯрем ва сӯҳбат мекунем.
paragraphs.en: In the evening we usually have dinner together and talk.

paragraphs.native: Баъзан мо телевизор тамошо мекунем, лекин зуд-зуд не, зеро вақти зиёд надорем.
paragraphs.en: Sometimes we watch TV, but not often, because we don't have much free time.

paragraphs.native: Ман мусиқиро дӯст медорам ва ҳар шаб пеш аз хоб чанд дақиқа гӯш мекунам.
paragraphs.en: I like music, and every night before bed I listen to it for a few minutes.

options: Khujand

lessons.title: A Visit to My Grandmother
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: a past-tense family visit

readingPassage.context: Simple past-tense narrative practicing everyday family vocabulary.

paragraphs.native: Дирӯз ман ба хонаи бибиям рафтам.
paragraphs.en: Yesterday I went to my grandmother's house.

paragraphs.native: Бибиям дар деҳа зиндагӣ мекунад.
paragraphs.en: My grandmother lives in a village.

paragraphs.native: Ман бо мошин ду соат роҳ рафтам.
paragraphs.en: I traveled two hours by car.

paragraphs.native: Бибиям маро бо табассум пешвоз гирифт.
paragraphs.en: My grandmother greeted me with a smile.

paragraphs.native: Вай барои ман нони гарм пухт.
paragraphs.en: She baked hot bread for me.

paragraphs.native: Мо дар ҳавлӣ якҷоя нишастем.
paragraphs.en: We sat together in the yard.

paragraphs.native: Бибиям дар бораи ҷавонияш ҳикоя кард.
paragraphs.en: My grandmother told a story about her youth.

paragraphs.native: Ман ба ҳикояҳояш бо диққат гӯш кардам.
paragraphs.en: I listened to her stories carefully.

paragraphs.native: Шабона ман дар хонаи бибиям хобидам.
paragraphs.en: At night I slept at my grandmother's house.

paragraphs.native: Субҳ ман бо дили нохоҳам ба хона баргаштам.
paragraphs.en: In the morning I reluctantly returned home.

lessons.title: The Best Season
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: family opinions about the seasons

readingPassage.context: Text practicing reasons/because-clauses and simple comparisons of preference.

paragraphs.native: Ҳар кас фасли дӯстдоштаи худро дорад.
paragraphs.en: Everyone has their own favorite season.

paragraphs.native: Ман баҳорро аз ҳама бештар дӯст медорам.
paragraphs.en: I love spring the most of all.

paragraphs.native: Дар баҳор ҳаво на хеле гарм, на хеле хунук аст.
paragraphs.en: In spring the weather is neither too hot nor too cold.

paragraphs.native: Дугонаам тобистонро дӯст медорад, зеро ӯ шино карданро дӯст медорад.
paragraphs.en: My friend loves summer because she loves swimming.

paragraphs.native: Бародарам тирамоҳро тарҷеҳ медиҳад, зеро ранги баргҳо зебост.
paragraphs.en: My brother prefers autumn because the color of the leaves is beautiful.

paragraphs.native: Падарам зимистонро дӯст медорад, зеро ӯ лижаронӣ мекунад.
paragraphs.en: My father loves winter because he skis.

paragraphs.native: Дар оилаи мо ҳар фасл тарафдори худро дорад.
paragraphs.en: In our family every season has its own supporter.

paragraphs.native: Мо ҳар сол дар бораи фасли беҳтарин баҳс мекунем.
paragraphs.en: Every year we argue about the best season.

paragraphs.native: Лекин дар охир, ҳама якдилона мегӯянд, ки ҳар фасл зебоии худро дорад.
paragraphs.en: But in the end, everyone unanimously says every season has its own beauty.

paragraphs.native: Ин баҳс ҳамеша бо ханда ба охир мерасад.
paragraphs.en: This argument always ends with laughter.

exercises.question: Which season does the narrator love most?
options: Summer

exercises.question: Why does the friend love summer?
options: She loves the sun
She loves swimming
She loves flowers

exercises.question: Why does the brother prefer autumn?
options: School starts

exercises.question: Why does the father love winter?
options: He skis

He likes snow only
He travels

exercises.question: What does the family do every year?
options: Travel together
Argue about the best season
Celebrate a holiday
Nothing special

exercises.question: How does the argument end?
options: In anger
With laughter
In silence
With a vote

lessons.title: My First Day of Work
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: starting a new job

readingPassage.context: Past-tense narrative practicing workplace vocabulary and emotional adjectives.

paragraphs.native: Моҳи гузашта ман кори навамро сар кардам.
paragraphs.en: Last month I started my new job.

paragraphs.native: Дар рӯзи аввал ман хеле нигарон будам.
paragraphs.en: On the first day I was very nervous.

paragraphs.native: Ҳамкорони нав бо ман хеле хуш муомила карданд.
paragraphs.en: My new colleagues treated me very kindly.

paragraphs.native: Роҳбарам ба ман вазифаҳоямро фаҳмонд.
paragraphs.en: My boss explained my duties to me.

paragraphs.native: Дар аввал ҳама чиз душвор менамуд.
paragraphs.en: At first everything seemed difficult.

paragraphs.native: Лекин баъд аз як ҳафта ман ба кор одат кардам.
paragraphs.en: But after a week I got used to the work.

paragraphs.native: Ҳамкорам ба ман бисёр чизҳои муфид ёд дод.
paragraphs.en: My colleague taught me many useful things.

paragraphs.native: Ҳоло ман кори худро дӯст медорам.
paragraphs.en: Now I love my job.

paragraphs.native: Ҳар субҳ бо хушҳолӣ ба идора меравам.
paragraphs.en: Every morning I go to the office happily.

paragraphs.native: Ман хушбахтам, ки ин кори наверо ёфтам.
paragraphs.en: I'm lucky that I found this new job.

exercises.question: When did the narrator start the new job?

exercises.question: How did the narrator feel on the first day?
options: Confident

options: Rudely

exercises.question: Who explained the duties?

exercises.question: When did the narrator get used to the work?

Never

exercises.question: How does the narrator feel now?
options: Still nervous

lessons.title: My Friend Farhod
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: a friendship, a career change, and a good conversation

readingPassage.context: A personal narrative reviewing past-tense storytelling and reported speech ("he told me that...") in everyday register.

paragraphs.native: Дар бораи дӯстам Фарход бигӯям.
paragraphs.en: Let me tell you about my friend Farhod.

paragraphs.native: Ӯ дар Хуҷанд таваллуд шудааст ва аллакай даҳ сол боз дар Душанбе зиндагӣ мекунад.
paragraphs.en: He was born in Khujand and has already lived in Dushanbe for ten years.

paragraphs.native: Пештар ӯ дар мактаб кор мекард, лекин ду сол пеш кори худро иваз кард ва ҳозир дар бонк кор мекунад.
paragraphs.en: He used to work at a school, but two years ago he changed his job, and now he works at a bank.

paragraphs.native: Ӯ ҳеҷ гоҳ хориҷа сафар накардааст, лекин мехоҳад рӯзе ба Туркия равад.
paragraphs.en: He has never traveled abroad, but he wants to go to Turkey someday.

paragraphs.native: Ҳафтаи гузашта мо якҷоя ба ресторан рафтем ва хӯроки хеле хуб хӯрдем.
paragraphs.en: Last week we went to a restaurant together and had a really good meal.

paragraphs.native: Ӯ ба ман гуфт, ки аллакай се моҳ боз забони англисиро меомӯзад ва ҳар рӯз як соат китоб мехонад.
paragraphs.en: He told me that he's already been studying English for three months and reads for an hour every day.

paragraphs.native: Ман ба ӯ гуфтам, ки соли гузашта ман дар Русия будам, лекин имсол ба он ҷо нарафтам.
paragraphs.en: I told him that last year I was in Russia, but I didn't go there this year.

paragraphs.native: Мо ду соат сӯҳбат кардем ва баъд ба хонаамон баргаштем.
paragraphs.en: We talked for two hours and then went back home.

lessons.title: Planning a Trip
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: modal verbs and travel planning

readingPassage.context: Text practicing бояд (must) / метавонад (can) and uncertainty markers in a travel-planning context.

paragraphs.native: Мо мехоҳем моҳи оянда ба кӯҳҳо сафар кунем.
paragraphs.en: We want to travel to the mountains next month.

paragraphs.native: Аввал бояд билетҳоро харем.
paragraphs.en: First we must buy tickets.

paragraphs.native: Баъд бояд меҳмонхонаро фармоем.
paragraphs.en: Then we must book a hotel.

paragraphs.native: Ман фикр мекунам, ки мо бояд либоси гарм гирем, зеро дар кӯҳҳо хунук аст.
paragraphs.en: I think we should take warm clothes because it's cold in the mountains.

paragraphs.native: Дӯстам гуфт, ки метавонем бо мошин равем ба ҷои тайёра.
paragraphs.en: My friend said we could go by car instead of plane.

paragraphs.native: Ин арзонтар аст, лекин вақти бештар мегирад.
paragraphs.en: This is cheaper, but takes more time.

paragraphs.native: Мо ҳанӯз тасмим нагирифтаем, ки чӣ хел равем.
paragraphs.en: We haven't yet decided how to go.

paragraphs.native: Агар ҳаво хуб бошад, метавонем пиёдагардӣ ҳам кунем.
paragraphs.en: If the weather is good, we can also go hiking.

paragraphs.native: Ман умедворам, ки ин сафар фаромӯшнашаванда хоҳад буд.
paragraphs.en: I hope this trip will be unforgettable.

paragraphs.native: Мо бо шавқи зиёд интизори сафар ҳастем.
paragraphs.en: We are waiting for the trip with great enthusiasm.

lessons.title: A Difficult Decision
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: weighing a job offer against family

readingPassage.context: Text practicing conditional clauses (агар... — if...) around a relatable life decision.

paragraphs.native: Дӯстам Наргис бояд қарори муҳимме қабул кунад.
paragraphs.en: My friend Nargis has to make an important decision.

paragraphs.native: Ба вай ду пешниҳоди корӣ доданд.
paragraphs.en: She was given two job offers.

paragraphs.native: Кори якум дар шаҳри худаш аст, лекин маош пасттар аст.
paragraphs.en: The first job is in her own city, but the salary is lower.

paragraphs.native: Кори дуюм дар шаҳри дигар аст, бо маоши баландтар.
paragraphs.en: The second job is in another city, with a higher salary.

paragraphs.native: Агар вай кори дуюмро қабул кунад, бояд аз оилааш дур зиндагӣ кунад.
paragraphs.en: If she accepts the second job, she must live far from her family.

paragraphs.native: Вай бо волидонаш маслиҳат кард, лекин онҳо низ мутмаин набуданд.
paragraphs.en: She consulted with her parents, but they weren't sure either.

paragraphs.native: Ман ба вай гуфтам, ки бояд ба дилаш гӯш кунад.
paragraphs.en: I told her that she should listen to her heart.

paragraphs.native: Дар ниҳоят, Наргис тасмим гирифт, ки дар шаҳри худаш бимонад.
paragraphs.en: In the end, Nargis decided to stay in her own city.

paragraphs.native: Вай гуфт, ки оила барояш аз пул муҳимтар аст.
paragraphs.en: She said family is more important to her than money.

paragraphs.native: Ман фикр мекунам, ки ин қарори дуруст буд.
paragraphs.en: I think this was the right decision.

exercises.question: What does Nargis have to make?
options: A cake
An important decision
A phone call
A trip

exercises.question: What is different about the two job offers?
options: Location and salary
Only the salary
Only the location
Working hours

exercises.question: What would happen if she took the second job?
options: She'd get a raise later
She'd have to live far from family
She'd work from home
Nothing would change

exercises.question: Who did she consult?
options: Her boss
Her parents
A stranger
Nobody

exercises.question: What advice did the narrator give?
options: Take the money
Listen to her heart
Ask a lawyer
Flip a coin

exercises.question: What did Nargis decide in the end?
options: To take the second job
To stay in her own city
To quit working
To ask for more time

lessons.title: Changing Habits
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: New Year's resolutions and small steps

readingPassage.context: Text practicing goal-setting language and reported advice from a friend.

paragraphs.native: Соли нав наздик мешавад, ва ман мехоҳам баъзе одатҳоямро тағйир диҳам.
paragraphs.en: The new year is approaching, and I want to change some of my habits.

paragraphs.native: Аввалан, ман мехоҳам бештар варзиш кунам.
paragraphs.en: First, I want to exercise more.

paragraphs.native: Ман ҳозир танҳо як маротиба дар ҳафта варзиш мекунам, лекин мехоҳам се маротиба кунам.
paragraphs.en: I currently exercise only once a week, but I want to do it three times.

paragraphs.native: Дуюм, ман мехоҳам камтар вақтро дар телефонам сарф кунам.
paragraphs.en: Second, I want to spend less time on my phone.

paragraphs.native: Ман фаҳмидам, ки ҳар рӯз якчанд соат бефоида телефонамро тамошо мекунам.
paragraphs.en: I realized that every day I waste several hours looking at my phone.

paragraphs.native: Ба ҷои он, ман мехоҳам бештар китоб хонам.
paragraphs.en: Instead, I want to read more books.

paragraphs.native: Дӯстам гуфт, ки тағйир додани одат душвор аст, лекин на ғайриимкон.
paragraphs.en: My friend said changing a habit is difficult, but not impossible.

paragraphs.native: Вай маслиҳат дод, ки бо қадамҳои хурд сар кунам.
paragraphs.en: She advised me to start with small steps.

paragraphs.native: Ман тасмим гирифтам, ки ҳар ҳафта як тағйироти хурд ворид кунам.
paragraphs.en: I decided to introduce one small change every week.

paragraphs.native: Умедворам, ки то охири сол ман шахси беҳтаре хоҳам буд.
paragraphs.en: I hope that by the end of the year I will be a better person.

lessons.title: A Busy Day at Home
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: a household juggling work, chores, and a homecoming

readingPassage.context: A slice-of-life narrative that leans on modal verbs (бояд — must, метавонад — can) and uncertainty markers (шояд — maybe) typical of B1-level connected speech.

paragraphs.native: Дар хонаи мо имрӯз ҳама банданд.
paragraphs.en: Everyone in our house is busy today.

paragraphs.native: Модарам бояд то соати панҷ дар корхона кор кунад, зеро баъзе фармоишҳо ҳанӯз тайёр карда нашудаанд.
paragraphs.en: My mother has to work at the factory until five o'clock because some orders haven't been finished yet.

paragraphs.native: Падарам метавонад мошин ронад ва аз ин рӯ ба фурудгоҳ рафт, то бародарамро пешвоз гирад — ӯ имшаб аз хориҷа мерасад.
paragraphs.en: My father can drive, so he went to the airport to pick up my brother — he's arriving from abroad tonight.

paragraphs.native: Ман бояд имрӯз хона тоза кунам, зеро пагоҳ меҳмонон меоянд.
paragraphs.en: I have to clean the house today because guests are coming tomorrow.

paragraphs.native: Шояд онҳо баъд аз нисфирӯзӣ расанд, лекин мутмаин нестам.
paragraphs.en: They might arrive in the afternoon, but I'm not sure.

paragraphs.native: Хоҳарам мехоҳад бо мо шом хӯрад, лекин вай гуфт, ки беҳтараш аввал дарсҳояшро тамом кунад.
paragraphs.en: My sister wants to have dinner with us, but she said she'd rather finish her homework first.

paragraphs.native: Фикр мекунам, ки мо бояд чизи хос тайёр кунем — эҳтимол палов ё шӯрбо.
paragraphs.en: I think we should make something special — maybe plov or soup.

paragraphs.native: Дар охир ҳама якҷоя менишинем ва дар бораи сафари бародарам сӯҳбат мекунем.
paragraphs.en: In the end we'll all sit together and talk about my brother's trip.

lessons.title: Working from Home
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: the pros and cons of remote work

readingPassage.context: Text weighing productivity claims and distraction concerns around remote work, in the connected-reasoning register typical of B2.

paragraphs.native: Дар солҳои охир, шумораи одамоне, ки аз хона кор мекунанд, хеле афзоиш ёфт.
paragraphs.en: In recent years, the number of people working from home has increased significantly.

paragraphs.native: Ин тарзи корро бисёриҳо тарафдорӣ мекунанд, зеро вақти сафарро сарфа мекунад.
paragraphs.en: Many people support this way of working because it saves commuting time.

paragraphs.native: Аз тарафи дигар, баъзеҳо мегӯянд, ки кор аз хона ба тамаркуз халал мерасонад.
paragraphs.en: On the other hand, some say that working from home disrupts concentration.

paragraphs.native: Дар хона омилҳои бисёре ҳастанд, ки метавонанд диққатро парешон кунанд — телевизор, фарзандон ва ё корҳои хонагӣ.
paragraphs.en: At home there are many factors that can distract attention — TV, children, or household chores.

paragraphs.native: Аммо тадқиқотҳо нишон медиҳанд, ки бисёр кормандон дар хона самараноктаранд.
paragraphs.en: But research shows that many employees are more productive at home.

paragraphs.native: Онҳо мегӯянд, ки бе халалрасонии ҳамкорон, метавонанд бо суръати худ кор кунанд.
paragraphs.en: They say that without colleagues' interruptions, they can work at their own pace.

paragraphs.native: Ширкатҳо ҳоло ба шакли омехта — якчанд рӯз дар хона, якчанд рӯз дар идора — рӯй меоранд.
paragraphs.en: Companies are now turning to a hybrid form — some days at home, some days at the office.

paragraphs.native: Ин шакл метавонад беҳтарин ҷанбаҳои ҳарду тарзи корро муттаҳид кунад.
paragraphs.en: This form can combine the best aspects of both ways of working.

paragraphs.native: Албатта, ин ҳама вобаста ба навъи кор ва хусусияти шахс аст.
paragraphs.en: Of course, all this depends on the type of work and the person's character.

paragraphs.native: Дар ниҳоят, ҳар шахс бояд тарзи мувофиқи худро пайдо кунад.
paragraphs.en: In the end, every person must find the way that suits them.

exercises.question: What has increased in recent years, per the text?
options: Office jobs
People working from home
Unemployment
Business travel

exercises.question: What do supporters of working from home value?
options: Free coffee
Saving commuting time
A private office
A big salary

exercises.question: What do critics say working from home disrupts?
options: Sleep
Concentration
Health
Income

exercises.question: What does research show about many employees at home?
options: They are less productive
They are more productive
Nothing changes
They quit sooner

exercises.question: What hybrid form are companies turning to?
options: Only office work
Only remote work
Some days at home, some at the office
Four-day weeks

exercises.question: What does the text say the choice ultimately depends on?
options: Company size
Type of work and the person's character
Salary only
Location only

lessons.title: The Value of Failure
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: reframing failure as part of growth

readingPassage.context: A reflective essay-style text on psychology and success, practicing conditional and reported-opinion structures.

paragraphs.native: Ҳеҷ кас аз шикаст хушҳол намешавад, лекин шикаст қисми муҳими рушди инсон аст.
paragraphs.en: No one is happy about failure, but failure is an important part of human growth.

paragraphs.native: Бисёр одамони муваффақ мегӯянд, ки онҳо пеш аз расидан ба муваффақият чандин маротиба шикаст хӯрдаанд.
paragraphs.en: Many successful people say they failed many times before reaching success.

paragraphs.native: Мушкилот дар он аст, ки бисёриҳо аз тарси шикаст ҳатто кӯшиш ҳам намекунанд.
paragraphs.en: The problem is that many people, out of fear of failure, don't even try.

paragraphs.native: Ин тарс метавонад имкониятҳои бузургро аз даст диҳонад.
paragraphs.en: This fear can cause great opportunities to be lost.

paragraphs.native: Психологҳо мегӯянд, ки муносибат ба шикаст муҳимтар аз худи шикаст аст.
paragraphs.en: Psychologists say the attitude toward failure is more important than the failure itself.

paragraphs.native: Агар шахс шикастро ҳамчун дарс бинад, вай метавонад аз он чизе биёмӯзад.
paragraphs.en: If a person sees failure as a lesson, they can learn something from it.

paragraphs.native: Аммо агар шахс шикастро ҳамчун ниҳоят бинад, вай метавонад умедашро гум кунад.
paragraphs.en: But if a person sees failure as an end, they can lose their hope.

paragraphs.native: Дар бисёр фарҳангҳо, шикаст боиси шарм дониста мешавад, ки ин муаммои калон аст.
paragraphs.en: In many cultures, failure is considered shameful, which is a big problem.

paragraphs.native: Тағйир додани ин тарзи фикр вақт мегирад, лекин муҳим аст.
paragraphs.en: Changing this way of thinking takes time, but it's important.

paragraphs.native: Дар охир, шахсоне, ки аз шикаст намеҳаросанд, эҳтимоли муваффақ шуданашон бештар аст.
paragraphs.en: In the end, people who don't fear failure are more likely to succeed.

exercises.question: What do many successful people say about failure?
options: They never failed
They failed many times before success
Failure should be avoided
Failure is rare

exercises.question: What is the problem the text identifies?
options: People fail too much
Fear of failure stops people from trying
Failure is too common
Nothing

exercises.question: What do psychologists say is more important than failure itself?
options: Money
The attitude toward failure
Luck
Talent

exercises.question: What happens if someone sees failure as a lesson?
options: They give up
They can learn from it
They get angry
Nothing changes

exercises.question: How is failure viewed in many cultures, per the text?
options: As a badge of honor
As shameful
As irrelevant
As funny

exercises.question: Who is more likely to succeed, per the closing line?
options: Those who avoid all risk
Those who don't fear failure
Those who are lucky
Those who never try

lessons.title: The Changing City
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: development versus heritage preservation

readingPassage.context: A text weighing urban growth against loss of historical character, a debate common to fast-growing cities across Central Asia.

paragraphs.native: Шаҳри ман дар бист соли охир ба таври азим тағйир ёфтааст.
paragraphs.en: My city has changed dramatically in the last twenty years.

paragraphs.native: Вақте ки ман кӯдак будам, дар ин ҷо бинои баланд хеле кам буд.
paragraphs.en: When I was a child, there were very few tall buildings here.

paragraphs.native: Ҳоло, бинокорони нав дар ҳама гӯшаву канор бунёд мешаванд.
paragraphs.en: Now, new buildings are being constructed on every corner.

paragraphs.native: Ин рушд бисёр ҷои кор ба вуҷуд овард, ки барои иқтисодиёт хуб аст.
paragraphs.en: This development created many jobs, which is good for the economy.

paragraphs.native: Лекин баъзе сокинони қадимӣ шикоят мекунанд, ки шаҳр рӯҳи қадимии худро гум кардааст.
paragraphs.en: But some old residents complain that the city has lost its old spirit.

paragraphs.native: Биноҳои таърихӣ баъзан бо биноҳои нав иваз мешаванд, ки ин таассуфовар аст.
paragraphs.en: Historical buildings are sometimes replaced with new ones, which is unfortunate.

paragraphs.native: Аз тарафи дигар, инфрасохтори нав зиндагиро осонтар кардааст.
paragraphs.en: On the other hand, new infrastructure has made life easier.

paragraphs.native: Роҳҳо беҳтар шудаанд, ва нақлиёти ҷамъиятӣ низ инкишоф ёфтааст.
paragraphs.en: Roads have improved, and public transportation has also developed.

paragraphs.native: Ман фикр мекунам, ки шаҳрҳо бояд рушд кунанд, лекин бояд гузаштаи худро низ ҳифз кунанд.
paragraphs.en: I think cities should develop, but they should also preserve their past.

paragraphs.native: Мутавозинии байни рушд ва ҳифзи мерос кори осон нест.
paragraphs.en: Balancing development and heritage preservation is not an easy task.

exercises.question: How has the city changed in twenty years?
options: It hasn't changed
It has changed dramatically
It has gotten smaller
It has become poorer

exercises.question: What did the development create, per the text?
options: Pollution only
Many jobs
Traffic only
Nothing positive

exercises.question: What do old residents complain about?
options: High prices
The city losing its old spirit
Too much traffic
Not enough jobs

exercises.question: What has improved due to new infrastructure?
options: Only roads
Roads and public transportation
Nothing
Only housing

exercises.question: What does the narrator think cities should do?
options: Never change
Develop while preserving the past
Only preserve old buildings
Only build new ones

exercises.question: How does the text describe balancing development and heritage?
options: Easy
Not an easy task
Impossible
Unnecessary

lessons.title: A Tight Deadline
lessons.description: Reading comprehension: a workplace crunch, resolved by teamwork

readingPassage.context: A workplace narrative using reported questions ("he asked whether...") and conditional consequence ("or the client would be unhappy"), representative of B2 connected reasoning.

paragraphs.native: Ҳафтаи гузашта дар кори ман ҳодисаи ҷолиб рӯй дод.
paragraphs.en: Something interesting happened at my job last week.

paragraphs.native: Роҳбарам гуфт, ки мо бояд то охири моҳ лоиҳаро тамом кунем, вагарна мизоҷ норозӣ хоҳад шуд.
paragraphs.en: My manager said that we had to finish the project by the end of the month, or the client would be unhappy.

paragraphs.native: Ман ба ҳамкорам гуфтам, ки набояд ин қадар нигарон шавад — мо метавонем кӯмак хоҳем ва ҳама чиз бо мушкилот ҳал мешавад.
paragraphs.en: I told my coworker that he shouldn't worry so much — we could ask for help and everything would get worked out.

paragraphs.native: Вай пурсид, ки оё ман фикр мекунам, ки мо ба вақт мерасем.
paragraphs.en: He asked whether I thought we'd make it in time.

paragraphs.native: Ман ҷавоб додам, ки умедворам, ки ҳа, лекин мутмаин нестам.
paragraphs.en: I answered that I hoped so, but I wasn't sure.

paragraphs.native: Мо тасмим гирифтем, ки якчанд рӯзи истироҳатро гузарем ва ҳар рӯз то дертар кор кунем.
paragraphs.en: We decided to skip a few days off and work later every day.

paragraphs.native: Дар ниҳоят, лоиҳа рӯзи ҷумъа тамом шуд ва мизоҷ хеле хушнуд буд.
paragraphs.en: In the end, the project was finished on Friday, and the client was very pleased.

paragraphs.native: Ҳама аз натиҷа хурсанд шуданд ва мо якҷоя онро ҷашн гирифтем.
paragraphs.en: Everyone was happy with the result, and we celebrated it together.

exercises.question: What did the manager say would happen if the project wasn't finished?
options: They would get promoted
The client would be unhappy
The office would close
They'd get a bonus

exercises.question: What did the narrator tell the coworker?
options: To quit
Not to worry so much
To ask the manager for more time
To work alone

exercises.question: What did the coworker ask?
options: Whether the client was angry
Whether they'd finish in time
Whether he could take a day off
Whether the manager was fair

exercises.question: What did they decide to do?
options: Hire more staff
Skip days off and work later
Ask for a deadline extension
Cancel the project

exercises.question: When was the project finished?
options: Monday
Wednesday
Friday
Sunday

exercises.question: How did everyone feel about the result?
options: Disappointed
Indifferent
Happy
Confused
